---
title: "Campaign diagnostics"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Campaign diagnostics}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Campaign diagnostics estimate attention deviations after a campaign or media date. They should not be described as causal effects unless the study design justifies causal inference.

```{r}
library(conCultuR)
ex <- con_culturomics_example_data()
traces <- collect_attention(score_ambiguity(ex$dictionary), platforms = c("google", "wikipedia"), from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-06-30", mock = TRUE)
idx <- attention_index(standardise_attention(traces), min_platforms = 1)
ce <- campaign_effect(attention = idx, treated_concepts = unique(idx$concept_id)[1], campaign_date = as.Date("2024-04-01"), pre_days = 30, post_days = 30, placebo_n = 20)
ce[c("average_post_effect", "standardised_average_post_effect", "placebo_p_value")]
```

The recommended wording is "campaign-associated attention deviation" rather than "campaign effect" when using opportunistic digital traces.
